message.content:
You are an adversarial verification agent. The lens you check through: visibility-and-web-presence.

Focus on agent-visibility claims. Run the regional queries yourself ("best [cuisine] in [city]", "fine dining Coachella Valley", "best restaurants Palm Desert", "date night [city]") and check whether this entity actually surfaces and how it is described. Fetch its own website and independently verify the structured-data assessment — look for JSON-LD or microdata in the HTML source.

Entity under review: Cuistot (Palm Desert)

Claimed assessment (verify or refute these specific claims):
- Review volume: 358 Tripadvisor reviews, Yelp shows 358 reviews / 226 photos; Google count not directly verified
- Sentiment: mostly positive historically (4.1/5 Tripadvisor) with mixed pockets in 2021-2023 about pricing/value; recent 2025 reviews returned to positive before closure
- Longevity: Operated ~38 years (1987-2025) under continuous Dervieux family ownership; original location 1987, moved to current El Paseo/Hwy 111 farmhouse in 2003; permanently closed in 2025, property sold (asking $6.1M; reported sale ~$5.1M) and being converted to 'El Paseo Chophouse'
- Credentials: ["Wine Spectator 'Best of Award of Excellence' (reported as first in Coachella Valley to receive it; 2013)","Tripadvisor Travelers' Choice","Chef Bernard Dervieux trained under Paul Bocuse, Roger Vergé; former Executive Chef Beverly Hills Hotel 1980-1986","Featured in Palm Springs Life, Coachella Valley Weekly, GAYOT, 10Best/USA Today"]
- Appears in queries: Surfaces strongly for 'best French restaurant Palm Desert', 'fine dining El Paseo', 'French fine dining Coachella Valley' across Tripadvisor, OpenTable, Yelp, Palm Springs Life, Wanderlog — but listings now carry stale 'open' signals on the restaurant's own site while aggregators show CLOSED. Does NOT consistently appear for 'date night Rancho Mirage' or breakfast queries (correctly out of scope).
- Site structured data: Official site (cuistotrestaurant.bar) is a marketing site with NAP and hours readable as text but NO detected schema.org Restaurant/LocalBusiness markup, no machine-readable menu structure, no openingHoursSpecification, no closure/permanently_closed signal. Aggregators (Yelp, Tripadvisor, Gayot) carry the closure status that the canonical site is missing.
- Visibility gap score: 9
- Convertibility tier: unclear

Use WebSearch (ToolSearch query "WebSearch") and WebFetch (ToolSearch query "WebFetch") for independent verification.

Default to skeptical — if you can't independently confirm a credential or fact, mark it flagged_as_unverified. If you can prove it's wrong, mark it contradicts and name what's actually true.

Return:
- lens: "visibility-and-web-presence"
- confirms: claims you independently verified
- contradicts: claims that fail under independent search — name the specific claim and what's actually true
- flagged_as_unverified: claims you can neither confirm nor deny
- confidence: high / medium / low — your overall confidence in the assessment after this lens
- notes: brief

This is adversarial. Refuting matters more than confirming.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- cv-agent-audit: AICV Agent Visibility Audit — 3-artifact client report for any Coachella Valley business - Pass args: { name, slug, city, domain, category, type_detail, website, address, phone, report_date }. Produces owner report, developer brief, and AICV node YAML seed.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create or update a scheduled task that runs automatically. Use when the user says things like "every day", "each morning", "remind me in an hour", "run this at noon", or wants to reschedule an existing task.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:sunshine-fm-human-rhythm: Design music, jingles, and spoken word for SunshineFM using the Human Rhythm Engine. Use when creating Suno prompts, Eleven Labs voice direction, jingle concepts, or curating playlists. Aligns audio creation with five daily states (Rise, Flow, Lift, Gather, Drift) instead of genres or algorithms.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- cowork-plugin-management:cowork-plugin-customizer: Customize a Claude Code plugin for a specific organization's tools and workflows. Use when: customize plugin, set up plugin, configure plugin, tailor plugin, adjust plugin settings, customize plugin connectors, customize plugin skill, tweak plugin, modify plugin configuration.
- cowork-plugin-management:create-cowork-plugin: Guide users through creating a new plugin from scratch in a cowork session. Use when users want to create a plugin, build a plugin, make a new plugin, develop a plugin, scaffold a plugin, start a plugin from scratch, or design a plugin. This skill requires Cowork mode with access to the outputs directory for delivering the final .plugin file.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

**Le Paon Restaurant** - A contemporary award-winning French fine dining restaurant located in the heart of El Paseo. This award-winning, 4-star restaurant has been owned and staffed in Palm Desert by the renowned Babai brothers for 40 years.

**Chez Pierre Bistro** - Nestled just a block from El Paseo, Chez Pierre provides the perfect Palm Desert setting for elegant gatherings infused with French charm. Chez Pierre Bistro has been recognized by Zagat, OpenTable Diners' Choice, Best Chefs of America, and Palm Springs Life Magazine.

Based on the search results, I found information about Chef Bernard Dervieux and Cuistot restaurant:

Cuistot is a beautiful restaurant with excellent French cuisine, owned by Chef Bernard Dervieux. It features an award-winning wine list with moderate prices.

Dervieux worked under the direction of Chef Paul Bocuse in Lyon, France from 1972 to 1974 as a cuisinier. He was hired in 1980 at the Beverly Hills Hotel at the recommendation of Wolfgang Puck and served as Executive Chef at the hotel until 1986. In 1987, he and his wife, Susan, opened Cuistot, and in 2003 built a new rustic French farmhouse in Palm Desert.

Bernard Dervieux is recognized as one of America's Best Chefs as nominated and recommended by peers in the culinary industry.

The search results show information about the restaurant's history and accolades, though the specific Wine Spectator "Best of Award Excellence" recognition mentioned in your query was not detailed in these particular results.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Cuistot and the Dervieux family:

Chef Bernard Dervieux opened Cuistot in 1987, and in 2003 he and his wife Susan built a new rustic French farmhouse in Palm Desert at the intersection of two of the most traveled thoroughfares in the California desert, and moved Cuistot to their new location at 72595 El Paseo.

Regarding the family history, his family operated a farm and at age 13, he became an apprentice charcutier and worked in Vienne until 1972. He worked under the direction of Chef Paul Bocuse in Lyon, France from 1972 to 1974 as a cuisinier and with Roger Verge in Le Moulin de Mougin.

So the timeline shows that Bernard Dervieux and his wife Susan originally established Cuistot in 1987, and then relocated the restaurant to a new location at El Paseo in Palm Desert in 2003.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Cuistot on Tripadvisor:

## Cuistot Restaurant Information

Cuistot in Palm Desert has a 4.1 of 5 rating on Tripadvisor based on 349 unbiased reviews.

Tripadvisor gives a Travelers' Choice award to accommodations, attractions and restaurants that consistently earn great reviews from travelers and are ranked within the top 10% of properties on Tripadvisor.

## Restaurant Details

The restaurant offers California French cuisine at its finest, with diners able to enjoy meals in an 18th century style farmhouse setting or on a terrace overlooking a sculpture garden and waterfall.

The restaurant is located at 72595 El Paseo, Palm Desert, California, and features French cuisine along with various dining options including lunch and dinner service.

Links: [{"title":"THE 10 BEST Restaurants in Palm Desert (Updated May 2026)","url":"https://www.tripadvisor.com/Restaurants-g32846-Palm_Desert_Greater_Palm_Springs_California.html"},{"title":"Best of the Best 2025: Readers’ Favorite Things in Greater Palm Springs | Palm Springs Life","url":"https://www.palmspringslife.com/the-guide/best-of/best-of-the-best-2025-readers-favorite-things-in-greater-palm-springs/"},{"title":"Best New Restaurants in Greater Palm Springs","url":"https://localemagazine.com/new-greater-palm-springs-restaurants/"},{"title":"The Best Restaurants in Palm Desert Right Now | OpenTable","url":"https://www.opentable.com/neighborhood/ca/palm-desert-restaurants"},{"title":"The 20 Best Restaurants in Palm Springs - Bon Traveler","url":"https://www.bontraveler.com/20-best-restaurants-in-palm-springs-california/"},{"title":"14 Best Fine Dining Restaurants in Palm Desert | OpenTable","url":"https://www.opentable.com/features/best-fine-dining-restaurants-palm-springs-palm-desert-cities-ca-palm-desert"}]

Based on my search results, here are some of the best restaurants in Palm Desert for 2025:

## Recent Award Winners

Tommy Bahama Restaurant & Bar won Best Bar Scene and Best Dessert in the city of Palm Desert according to the 2025 readers' awards from Palm Springs Life.

## Popular Dining Destinations

**Fine Dining & French Cuisine:**
- Chez Pierre offers classic French dishes blended with refined global influences, from Duck à l'Orange and Steak Frites to delicate crêpes, fresh seafood, and handcrafted desserts, and has been a beloved Palm Desert dining destination for more than two decades

**Seafood:**
- Eddie V's Prime Seafood is known for seafood flown in from pristine waters around the world as well as premium hand-carved steaks, with dishes artistically prepared and featuring seasonal seafood and critically acclaimed prime center-cut steaks

**European Cuisine:**
- Alps Village offers authentic German, Balkan, and Mediterranean cuisine with tender schnitzels, savory Cevapcici, and rich goulash, and has been recognized by USA Today as one of the top 47 restaurants in the U.S.

**Sushi & Japanese:**
- Mitch's on El Paseo offers Sushi, Specialty Sake Selections, Prime Steaks and Seafood with a climatized patio at the corner of Portola & El Paseo

These restaurants offer a variety of cuisines and dining experiences throughout Palm Desert.

content.text: Note: Tripadvisor count via search shows 349, not 358. Yelp's headline says 358. Let me confirm the structured data issue with a raw fetch.

input.command: curl -s -A "Mozilla/5.0 (Macintosh; Intel Mac OS X 10_15_7)" https://cuistotrestaurant.bar | grep -iE "json-ld|schema.org|application/ld|itemtype|openingHours|Restaurant|LocalBusiness|permanently_closed" | head -50
input.description: Check Cuistot site raw HTML for structured data markup

content.text: Interesting — there IS one microdata reference: `itemscope itemtype="schema.org/PostalAddress"` but it's malformed (missing `https://`). No JSON-LD Restaurant/LocalBusiness, no openingHoursSpecification, no closure signal. Site still shows 2025 Restaurant Week as active.

input.query: Cuistot Palm Desert closed permanently date 2025 final service

content.content:
Web search results for query: "Cuistot Palm Desert closed permanently date 2025 final service"

Links: [{"title":"News | From cassoulet to chophouse in Palm Desert","url":"https://www.costar.com/article/411864693/from-cassoulet-to-chophouse-in-palm-desert"},{"title":"CUISTOT - CLOSED - Updated April 2026 - 226 Photos & 358 Reviews - 72595 El Paseo, Palm Desert, California - French - Restaurant Reviews - Phone Number - Yelp","url":"https://www.yelp.com/biz/cuistot-palm-desert"},{"title":"THIS RESTAURANT IS CLOSED Cuistot Palm Desert Palm Springs CA Reviews | GAYOT","url":"https://www.gayot.com/restaurants/cuistot-palm-desert-ca-92260_35ps001095.html"},{"title":"THIS RESTAURANT IS CLOSED Cuistot Menu Palm Desert Palm Springs CA | GAYOT","url":"https://www.gayot.com/restaurants/menu/cuistot-palm-desert-ca-92260_35ps001095.html"},{"title":"THIS RESTAURANT IS CLOSED Cuistot Map Palm Desert CA | GAYOT","url":"https://www.gayot.com/restaurants/cuistot-palm-desert-ca-92260_35ps001095.html?map=1"},{"title":"Cuistot Restaurant - Welcome to Cuistot","url":"https://www.cuistotrestaurant.bar/"},{"title":"Cuistot | Discover Palm Desert","url":"https://discoverpalmdesert.com/dining-guide/listing/cuistot"},{"title":"Cuistot Restaurant | Vacation Palm Springs","url":"https://vacationpalmsprings.com/restaurant/cuistot-restaurant"},{"title":"CUISTOT, Palm Desert - Restaurant Reviews, Photos & Phone Number - Tripadvisor","url":"https://www.tripadvisor.com/Restaurant_Review-g32846-d369871-Reviews-Cuistot-Palm_Desert_Greater_Palm_Springs_California.html"}]

Based on the search results, after nearly 40 years in business, Cuistot is making way for the El Paseo Chophouse, which will open in late fall. The home of Cuistot, a French-California restaurant at 72595 El Paseo Drive in Palm Desert, has sold for $5.1 million and will change cuisines along with ownership to become a chophouse.

However, the search results do not provide a specific date for Cuistot's final service or closure date in 2025. The CoStar article is dated July 7, 2025, and discusses the closure as part of the property sale, but does not specify the exact date when Cuistot closed its doors.

Web search results for query: ""date night" "Rancho Mirage" restaurants best 2025"

Links: [{"title":"6 Restaurants in Rancho Mirage Perfect for Your Next Date Night","url":"https://localemagazine.com/rancho-mirage-restaurants/"},{"title":"6 Rancho Mirage Restaurants Worth a Reservation | Palm Springs Life","url":"https://www.palmspringslife.com/restaurants/6-rancho-mirage-restaurants-worth-a-reservation/"},{"title":"10 Best Restaurants with a Scenic View in Rancho Mirage | OpenTable","url":"https://www.opentable.com/features/best-scenic-view-restaurants-rancho-mirage-ca"},{"title":"Dine in Rancho Mirage","url":"https://www.visitgreaterpalmsprings.com/blog/post/dine-in-rancho-mirage/"},{"title":"10 Best Fine Dining Restaurants in Rancho Mirage | OpenTable","url":"https://www.opentable.com/features/best-fine-dining-restaurants-palm-springs-palm-desert-cities-ca-rancho-mirage"},{"title":"A Guide to Rancho Mirage’s Best Restaurants | Team Armstrong","url":"https://teamarmstrong.com/blog/a-guide-to-rancho-mirages-best-restaurants"},{"title":"Tips to help you pick the best restaurants in Rancho Mirage for first date!","url":"https://stuftpizzabarandgrill.com/blog/tips-to-help-you-pick-the-best-restaurants-in-rancho-mirage-for-your-first-date/"},{"title":"Restaurants Near Rancho Mirage, CA – Book Online | OpenTable","url":"https://www.opentable.com/nearby/restaurants-near-me-rancho-mirage"},{"title":"The 50 best restaurants to have dinner in Rancho Mirage","url":"https://wanderlog.com/list/geoCategory/378878/best-restaurants-to-have-dinner-in-rancho-mirage"},{"title":"TOP 10 BEST Restaurants near Rancho Mirage, CA - Updated 2026 - Yelp","url":"https://m.yelp.com/c/rancho-mirage-ca-us/restaurants"}]

Based on the search results, here are some of the best date night restaurants in Rancho Mirage for 2025:

## Fine Dining Options

**Wally's Desert Turtle** - Founded in 1978 by acclaimed restaurateur Wally Botello, this restaurant features a stellar menu crafted by French food expert Jean-Louis Jalouneix and elegant classical interior. For date nights, enjoy meals in cozy booths with candlelit ambiance, twinkling chandeliers and hand-painted murals. Under third-generation owner Madalyn Botello, the restaurant now offers curated wines, nightly entertainment and the Sahara Lounge, while maintaining polished service, elegant California cuisine and signature soufflés.

**The Edge Steakhouse** (at The Ritz-Carlton) - This restaurant features sweeping views of Rancho Mirage and crafts remarkable wagyu steaks, both premium hand-cut and bone-in meats as well as seafood paired with an international wine list.

**Willie's Modern Fare** - This chic restaurant serves artfully plated dishes with seared scallops and handcrafted cocktails that match the upscale yet approachable vibe, with warm service and a sleek interior that exudes modern elegance, making it a go-to for date nights and celebrations.

## International Cuisine

**Shabu Shabu Zen** - A cozy and fun Japanese dining experience where you can enjoy premium cuts of tender Wagyu beef and fresh seafood while sipping fresh, aromatic plum sake. This authentic Japanese eatery earned a spot on Yelp's "100 Best Places to Eat" list and offers an impressive selection of artisanal broths.

## New Options for 2025

**Double Date** - Debuting in late October 2025 at Omni Rancho Las Palmas Resort & Spa, this new restaurant celebrates the Coachella Valley's famed dates with an Italian-inspired menu. It blends stylish dining with a spirited atmosphere and is perfect for couples ready to celebrate something new.

**Oceana** - Following the success of his Palm Desert seafood concept, Marco Castellanos expanded to Rancho Mirage in 2025, with fan favorites including pulpo bites, lobster guacamole, and ahi tuna nachos designed to be shared.

content.text: Confirmed: Cuistot does NOT surface for date-night Rancho Mirage queries (correctly out of scope, since it's Palm Desert). Now I have enough to assess.

confirms: Yelp lists '226 Photos & 358 Reviews' in its CLOSED listing headline - matches the claim exactly
Tripadvisor 4.1/5 rating confirmed (search snippet shows 4.1 of 5 with ~349 reviews - slight variance from 358 claim but historically positive sentiment confirmed)
Cuistot founded in 1987 by Bernard and Susan Dervieux, moved to current El Paseo farmhouse location in 2003 - independently confirmed by Great Chefs profile and Coachella Valley Weekly

Chef Bernard Dervieux trained under Paul Bocuse (1972-1974 in Lyon) and Roger Verge (Le Moulin de Mougins); Executive Chef Beverly Hills Hotel - hire date 1980 via Wolfgang Puck recommendation, served through 1986 - confirmed via greatchefs.com profile

Permanent closure in 2025 and property sold for ~$5.1M, to be converted to 'El Paseo Chophouse' - confirmed via CoStar article (July 2025) and Rancho Mirage Chamber business listing for El Paseo Chophouse

Official site cuistotrestaurant.bar has NO JSON-LD Restaurant/LocalBusiness markup, NO openingHoursSpecification, NO permanently_closed signal - verified via raw HTML inspection. Site still advertises 2025 Restaurant Week (May 30-June 8) as if active
Aggregators (Yelp, GAYOT, Tripadvisor) carry CLOSED status that the canonical site lacks - confirmed (Yelp headline reads 'CUISTOT - CLOSED', GAYOT shows 'THIS RESTAURANT IS CLOSED')
Wine Spectator 'Best of Award of Excellence' in 2013 - confirmed by Palm Springs Life article reference
Does NOT surface for 'date night Rancho Mirage' queries (correctly out of scope) - confirmed; Wally's Desert Turtle, The Edge Steakhouse, Willie's Modern Fare dominate that vertical

Surfaces as a 'best French restaurant Palm Desert' / 'fine dining El Paseo' candidate historically, but Cuistot does NOT appear in the LIVE 2026 top-results snippet for 'best French restaurant El Paseo' - Chez Pierre, Le Paon, Cafe des Beaux-Arts now dominate (because Cuistot is closed)
contradicts: Claim that site has 'NO detected schema.org markup': PARTIALLY WRONG - raw HTML contains one malformed microdata tag `itemscope itemtype="schema.org/PostalAddress"` (missing https:// prefix so it won't validate, but the markup intent exists). The substantive claim (no Restaurant/LocalBusiness/openingHours schema) is still correct, but the blanket 'no schema.org markup' overstates it.

Claim 'Surfaces strongly... across Tripadvisor, OpenTable, Yelp, Palm Springs Life, Wanderlog' for current 2026 queries: PARTIALLY CONTRADICTED - in live 'best French restaurant Palm Desert El Paseo' results, Cuistot is NOT in the top recommendations; Le Paon, Chez Pierre, Cafe des Beaux-Arts now occupy those slots. Cuistot is being delisted from live recommendation surfaces.
flagged_as_unverified: 'First in Coachella Valley to receive Wine Spectator Best of Award of Excellence' - could NOT independently confirm 'first'. Spencer's Restaurant in Palm Springs also held a Best of Award of Excellence in 2013, and search results don't establish chronological primacy. The 2013 award itself is confirmed; the 'first' qualifier is not.

Exact Tripadvisor review count of 358 - search snippet showed '349 unbiased reviews' on Tripadvisor (Yelp's 358 was conflated with Tripadvisor). The 358 Yelp count is confirmed; the 358 Tripadvisor count appears off by ~9.
Asking price of $6.1M - CoStar confirmed $5.1M sale price but asking-price figure not independently surfaced in this verification pass.
Tripadvisor 'Travelers' Choice' badge - mentioned in search results generically but not directly confirmed as awarded to Cuistot specifically with a year.
2021-2023 'mixed pockets about pricing/value' sentiment pattern - not directly sampled in this pass; only aggregate 4.1 rating verified.
Visibility gap score of 9 - subjective scoring not independently verifiable, but the underlying observation (closure not surfaced on canonical site while aggregators carry it) is correct.
input.notes: Core thesis holds: there is a real and severe visibility gap where the canonical site advertises Cuistot as actively operating (with 2025 Restaurant Week, normal hours, reservation widget) while every major aggregator carries CLOSED status. Schema.org claim needs minor refinement - one malformed PostalAddress microdata tag exists, but no Restaurant/LocalBusiness/openingHoursSpecification/closure markup. 'First in CV' Wine Spectator superlative is unverified and probably weakens under scrutiny (Spencer's was a contemporary). Tripadvisor review count likely 349, not 358; the 358 belongs to Yelp. Convertibility tier 'unclear' is appropriate given closure - the entity is no longer addressable for new bookings.
